metadata.title:
DESTITUTE MEN ARE


+. SENT; FIND NO WORK

PITIFUL CONDITIONS ARE SHOWN HERE
Families Arrive From East---Only
Jobs Available Are Those of


Strikebreakers.

pages.raw_text:
There Is evidence to show that
Che Industrial Removal office of
New York is playing into the hands
Of the railroads and the Western
Manufacturers association, in spite
Of previous denia,s. BY sending
men west without resources, during
the dull season of the year, and
obIiging them to work for low
wages or starve, it is believed that
that association, in league with the
railroads and the western manu-
facturers, Is striving to bring
about a reduction Of wages in the
west, and to take the bread from
the mouths Of the workinsmen who
are already here.


Developments of the most heart
rending character have taken place
in the cases of the 26 Hebrew work
ers who, during the last few
months have been sent to Spokane
through the Industrial Removal
office of New York. Actual starva-
ton, nearly causing the death of
one of the victims, sickness, un-
sanitary and inhuman living condi-
tions form part of the sickening
story told by these Hebrews to a
representative of The Press last
night.


These men, with their families,
were sent out here from New York.
They are tradesmen, carpenters.
shoemakers, paperhangers, brick
layers. Their fare to Spokane was
maid They had little money but
were promised that upon their ar
rived at Spokane they would be
given work.


NO WORK FOR THEM.


Upon their arrival here. they
were not given work, but told to
get job by working for lower
wages than is customarily received
here, or to work as strikebreakers
in the local Northern Pacific yards.
